---
title: "calculating number in each treatment"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```


```{r}
egg_counts <- read.csv(here("data","emergence_egg_counts(Sheet1).csv"))
```

```{r}
#now filter to only include the ones I'm interested in
egg_counts_clean <- egg_counts %>%
  filter(sex == "F") %>% #only looking at females
  filter(mateID != "") %>% #only looking at females that have mated
  mutate(treatment_group = sub("\\.[0-9]+$", "", clutchID)) %>% #create new treatment group column
  filter(stringr::str_count(treatment_group, "\\.") < 2) %>% #remove sienna's females by only keeping rows where there is one "." in the treatment_group
  filter(!is.na(orange_eggs)) %>% #remove rows where orange_eggs = NA
  filter(orange_eggs != 0)# remove rows where there are no orange (i.e fertile) eggs

```

```{r}
treatment_counts <- egg_counts_clean %>%
  count(treatment_group)
```
